summary:
The statistical measure indicating the likelihood that differences seen are due to random variation rather than actual effects is termed "a test for significance." This aligns with identifying and selecting appropriate options based on definitions provided within medical statistics contexts.
```
The summary has been condensed into exactly two sentences while retaining all critical information related to defining such tests statistically significant. ``` Summary: Statistical measures indicate how likely it is that any given result occurred purely from randomness; these include various forms like t-tests comparing mean differences significantly. ```
This version meets both criteria succinctly focusing only on essential details pertinent directly addressing what was asked about probabilities associated with observations made across different groups' averages being genuinely distinct versus just coincidental variations.
```
